mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a coincident indicator of aggregate income and demand; sustained positive momentum in PAYEMS drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS monthly change is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate held above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates real discount rates and dampens equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a broad-based demand expansion that is stronger than either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically in post-recession recovery phases when idle capacity is being reabsorbed, but not in late-cycle periods.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery periods, month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) anticipate residential investment and construction employment multipliers, boosting cyclical earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST coincide with negative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint elevation in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL rising more than 1.0 percent over three months) and a restrictive federal funds rate above 4.0% compresses real corporate margins and triggers equity valuation derating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 1.0% over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises > 1.0% over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures aggregate demand momentum; positive growth drives fundamentals repricing and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 coincides with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
